title_en: Salah: The Seventeen Pillars and the Conditions of Prayer
content_en:
The prayer chapter in Mughni al-Muhtaj is one of the most comprehensive treatments of salah in any single Shafi'i work, covering in detail the conditions, pillars, obligatory elements, recommended acts, invalidators, and special prayers (Friday, Eid, prayers in congregation) that constitute the full landscape of Shafi'i prayer law.

Ash-Shirbini begins by explaining the Shafi'i classification of what is required for salah into shurut (conditions that must be met before the prayer begins) and arkan (pillars that must be performed within the prayer). The conditions include: Islam, mental competence, distinguishing age, knowledge that the prayer time has entered, taharah from both ritual impurity (hadath) and physical impurity (najasah), covering the awrah, facing the qiblah, and standing if physically capable. Each condition is explained with its evidence and its consequences if absent or uncertain.

The seventeen pillars (arkan) of prayer that an-Nawawi enumerates — and which ash-Shirbini explains — represent the Shafi'i school's comprehensive analysis of what the Prophet's prayer consisted of as demonstrated practice. They are: the opening takbir (Allahu Akbar); standing in obligatory prayer while capable; reciting Surah al-Fatiha in every rak'ah; ruku' (bowing until the back is level); remaining still in ruku' (tuma'ninah); rising from ruku' to full standing; remaining still in the standing after ruku'; two prostrations in each rak'ah; remaining still in each prostration; sitting between the two prostrations; remaining still in that sitting; the final tashahhud; sitting for the final tashahhud; the salawat upon the Prophet in the final sitting; and the two final salams.

The tuma'ninah (tranquility/stillness) requirement is a pillar in the Shafi'i school. This means remaining in each position — ruku', sujud, qiyam after ruku', and sitting between prostrations — for at least the duration of one complete SubhanAllah. The hadith basis is the famous hadith of the man who prayed poorly, reported in Bukhari and Muslim, in which the Prophet said: 'Return and pray, for you have not prayed.' The man was rushing through the prayer without proper stillness, and the Prophet's command to repeat indicates that the prayer was invalid.

Ash-Shirbini gives special attention to the recitation of Surah al-Fatiha, which is a pillar in every rak'ah according to the Shafi'i school — including the third and fourth rak'ahs of four-rak'ah prayers. This position, based on the hadith 'There is no prayer for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book,' is the most demanding of the four schools on this point. The Hanafi school requires Fatiha in the first two rak'ahs only; the Maliki and Hanbali positions are closer to the Shafi'i view. Ash-Shirbini examines the evidence for all positions.

The opening supplication (du'a al-istiftah) and the seeking of refuge (ta'awwudh) are sunnahs that precede the Fatiha. The basmala (Bismillah ar-Rahman ar-Rahim) is held by the Shafi'i school to be a verse of al-Fatiha and must be recited as part of it — said aloud in the loud prayers (Fajr, Maghrib, Isha). This position distinguishes the Shafi'i school from the Hanafi (which says basmala quietly) and aligns partly with the Hanbali school.

The chapter on congregational prayer is extensive in Mughni al-Muhtaj. Following the imam is obligatory for men who can do so without undue hardship, according to the Shafi'i school's strong encouragement of jama'ah prayer. The conditions for the validity of following an imam — that the follower must not be in a higher position of obligation than the imam, must be able to see or hear the imam, must not precede the imam in any pillar — are all explained with their evidential basis and practical implications.
title_id: Shalat: Tujuh Belas Rukun dan Syarat-Syarat Shalat
content_id:
Bab shalat dalam Mughni al-Muhtaj adalah salah satu perlakuan paling komprehensif tentang shalat dalam karya Syafi'i tunggal mana pun, mencakup secara terperinci syarat-syarat, rukun-rukun, kewajiban-kewajiban, perbuatan-perbuatan yang dianjurkan, dan perbuatan-perbuatan yang membatalkan shalat yang menjadi ciri khas analisis shalat Syafi'i. Asy-Syirbini mengikuti formulasi an-Nawawi tentang tujuh belas rukun shalat dan menyediakan penjelasan yang terperinci tentang masing-masing, mengklarifikasi apa yang dibutuhkan oleh setiap rukun dan apa yang tidak.

Penjelasan asy-Syirbini tentang niat sebagai rukun pertama shalat sangat rinci, menangani pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang isi yang tepat dari niat, waktu dan cara membentuknya, dan implikasi dari niat yang cacat atau tidak lengkap. Madzhab Syafi'i membutuhkan niat yang lebih eksplisit daripada beberapa madzhab lain, dan komentar asy-Syirbini membantu pembaca memahami persis apa yang dibutuhkan dan mengapa.

Perlakuan terhadap bagian-bagian shalat yang diperdebatkan — apakah penyebutan Bismillah di awal Al-Fatihah wajib atau sunnah, bagaimana cara mengucapkan tasyahud dengan benar, dan kapan sujud sahwi diperlukan — didasarkan pada referensi ke sumber-sumber hadis dan penilaian para ahli Syafi'i dengan cara yang membuat dasar-dasar posisi madzhab terlihat jelas. Asy-Syirbini sangat kuat dalam menjelaskan tidak hanya apa posisi madzhab tetapi mengapa madzhab mengambil posisi itu dibandingkan alternatif-alternatif yang tersedia.
content_ar:
في كتاب "مغني المحتاج إلى معرفة معاني ألفاظ المنهاج"، يستعرض هذا الجزء أبوابًا من أبواب الفقه الشافعي بشرح وافٍ ومناقشة دقيقة مما له صلة بالموضوع المعنون أعلاه، مستعينًا بالأدلة الشرعية من الكتاب والسنة وأقوال الأئمة والعلماء.

ويُفصّل المؤلف في هذا القسم المسائل الواردة تفصيلًا وافيًا، مع الإشارة إلى وجوه الخلاف بين العلماء ومواضع الاتفاق، مُستندًا إلى منهج أهل العلم في التحقيق والتدقيق والاستدلال بالدليل الصحيح.

ويُركّز المؤلف على استيعاب الفروع الفقهية والمسائل العلمية المتعلقة بهذا الموضوع، موضّحًا الحِكَم والعلل الشرعية حيث أمكن، مما يُعين القارئ على الفهم الصحيح والتطبيق السليم لما يتعلمه من أحكام ومسائل.

ويختتم هذا الجزء بخلاصة جامعة للمسائل التي تناولها المؤلف، مع الإشارة إلى المراجع والمصادر التي استقى منها معلوماته، وهو ما يُعزز الثقة بمضمون الكتاب ويجعله مرجعًا موثوقًا في بابه.
